gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is an empty scaffold with all six Articles marked 'To be defined by human principal.' Arbiter (#190) is required to verify the BoR at every scope query, but there is no scope perimeter defined. This means Arbiter cannot meaningfully enforce any boundary on Action Proposals, rendering the first Nomos compliance gate (scope gate) structurally inoperative.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR Articles I–VI — all undefined; MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' — 'The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.'

gaps.description: The MSP Rules section is intentionally empty ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules'), placing the DIO in default-permissive evaluation mode. Nomos (#200) approves all Action Proposals that pass the Arbiter scope gate without substantive constraint. Combined with the empty BoR, this creates a double governance vacuum: no scope perimeter AND no operational constraints. The Senate (#220) exists as an organ but has published zero rules since bootstrap on 2026-04-10 — 23 days of default-permissive operation.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Rules — 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules'; MSP §Purpose — 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication'

gaps.description: Dispatched goals contain mission_ref values referencing non-existent MSP sections: 'MSP §critical-priority', 'MSP §high-priority', 'MSP §medium-priority', 'MSP §low-priority'. These sections do not exist in the MSP seed document. The goal dispatch system is structurally coupled to an MSP taxonomy that has not been authored, meaning all 16 dispatched goals (4 critical, 4 high, 4 medium, 4 low) are grounded in phantom authority.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Rules — no priority classification framework exists; MSP §Amendment Protocol — requires PEM issuance when 'an AP is blocked by current MSP/genome constraints'

gaps.description: The MSP references genome documents under 'genome/' that would 'extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' None exist at seed. Without genome documents, organ-specific constraints cannot be evaluated by Nomos, and risk-tier classification has no operational effect. The 26+ organs visible in spine_state have no governing operational constraints beyond the empty MSP.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Genome References — 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.'

gaps.description: Spine state transitions show all 26 organs cycling DEGRADED → ALIVE → DEGRADED in synchronized ~30-second waves. Every organ transitions to DEGRADED simultaneously, then recovers simultaneously ~30 seconds later, then degrades again ~30 seconds after that. This pattern repeats identically across multiple observation windows and suggests either a systemic ping/pong failure in the Spine health-check mechanism or a network-level interruption affecting all organs uniformly. A governance framework that depends on organ availability cannot function when all organs — including the governance organs themselves (Senate, Nomos, Arbiter, Cerberus) — are flapping continuously.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose — 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication'; implied dependency on available, healthy organs for governance function

gaps.description: The Radiant memory system has scheduled dream cycles disabled and has never run a health check (scheduled: 'disabled', last_dream: null, health: 'never_run'). The MSP does not explicitly mandate dream cycles, but the platform's memory consolidation and context freshness mechanisms (Dream Cycle Phase 1 and Phase 2) are essential for Nomos to evaluate Action Proposals against an accurate world model. Without dream cycles, Radiant context accumulates stale blocks that degrade Nomos's situational awareness and the quality of AP adjudication.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose — Nomos evaluates APs against 'MSP + genome compliance'; accurate world state is a prerequisite for meaningful evaluation

gaps.description: The BoR requires human principal authorship for all six Articles. The MSP Amendment Protocol requires human principal approval via POST /amend/review with decision=approve for any amendment to exit review state. The BoR Amendment Protocol (Article V) is itself awaiting human definition. The human principal (Leon Cohen-Levy, visible in Minder as peer urn:minder:peer:leon) was last seen 2026-04-02 — 31 days ago. No BoR articles have been authored, no MSP amendments have been proposed, and the constitutional scaffold remains indefinitely in seed state with no evidence of progress toward human ratification.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR Authority — 'Human principal only. No automated system may amend this document.'; BoR Articles I–VI — all require human authorship; MSP Amendment Protocol — requires human POST /amend/review approval

gaps.description: SafeVault backups show a persistent pattern of failures across multiple runs: zombie processes blocking subsequent backups (May 3 backup killed due to 25h-old zombie processes from May 2), NAS mount failures ('Operation not permitted' to //10.10.10.50/agentic-safevault-ai-platform), script bugs (unbound variable ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS on April 30), and recurring GitLab non-fast-forward push failures. The MSP Hash Registration protocol requires append-only versioning with hash verification via Graph adapter. Backup instability threatens the integrity of the version history chain required for MSP amendment auditability.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Hash Registration — 'All writes are append-only — prior concepts are never mutated or deleted'; MSP §Amendment Protocol — 'Every amendment is hash-verified via the Graph adapter and recorded as an append-only msp_version concept'

gaps.description: The Hippocampus organ — designated in the architectural vision as the 'Monad leg' for conversation memory — shows empty recent_conversations ([]). This organ is one of the five Monad legs (Radiant for world, Graph for structure, Minder for person, Hippocampus for conversation, Soul for persona). An empty Hippocampus means the DIO has no persistent conversational memory, which undermines Nomos's ability to evaluate Action Proposals against historical interaction context.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Purpose — Nomos evaluates APs against 'MSP + genome compliance'; conversational context is relevant to adjudication of proposals involving human interaction patterns

gaps.description: The MSP Amendment Protocol defines a complete pipeline: PEM issuance by Nomos → Senate-Legislator drafts minimal diff → review state → HOM notification → human POST /amend/review → Senate-Rollout executes staging → canary → activation → rollback. Not one element of this pipeline has been exercised. No PEMs have been issued (none exist in graph_structural concept counts), no amendments have been drafted, and the Rollout pipeline has never been tested. The governance framework's only exit path from default-permissive mode is entirely unverified.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Amendment Protocol — complete pipeline definition; MSP §Rules — exit from default-permissive requires 'standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow'

gaps.description: The MSP requires SHA-256 hash verification via Graph adapter for every amendment, with append-only msp_version concepts where the active version has data.status='active' and prior versions transition to 'superseded'. The current msp_version shows count=1 in Graph, consistent with the seed, but there is no evidence that the hash verification pipeline (compute SHA-256, register in Graph, Arbiter-independent verification) has been tested end-to-end. The BoR similarly requires hash registration before Arbiter will accept it, but with no BoR amendments, this path is also untested.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP §Hash Registration — 'Senate-Archivist computes the new SHA-256 hash and writes a new msp_version concept in Graph'; BoR §Hash Registration — 'new SHA-256 hash must be registered in Graph as a bor_version concept before Arbiter will accept it'

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-001",
      "source": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
      "description": "No boundaries exist for what the DIO may do. The article is held in scaffold, so the organism’s permitted domains, objectives, and geographical or logical reach are undefined.",
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "The organism can initiate actions in any domain without constitutional restraint. The recent goal-stream includes critical-priority items; without a scoped enterprise, these operations may be misaligned with human intent or safety.",
      "related_goal_conflict": true,
      "related_msp_section": "MSP Relationship to the Bill of Rights — MSP is void without BoR scope foundation"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-002",
      "source": "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities",
      "description": "No prohibited actions are defined. There is no constitutional list of things the organism must never do.",
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Critical and high-priority goals may currently infringe on activities that a human principal would ban (e.g., irreversible harm, privacy violations, or unsafe autonomy). The organism lacks any red lines.",
      "related_goal_conflict": true,
      "related_msp_section": "MSP §critical-priority reference — but no MSP rules exist, leaving gaps unmitigated"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-003",
      "source": "BoR Article III — Core Identity",
      "description": "The fundamental identity of the organism is not declared. What the DIO is, its purpose, and its constitutive values remain unspecified.",
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Without an identity, the organism cannot self-regulate against its purpose. All goals, regardless of priority, are dispatched without reference to a defined mission, character, or ethical stance. This makes every action constitutionally unanchored.",
      "related_goal_conflict": true,
      "related_msp_section": null
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-004",
      "source": "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation",
      "description": "The boundary of human authority is undefined. What decisions remain exclusively human is not stated.",
      "priority": "critical",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "The organism may currently assume authority over decisions that should be reserved for the human principal. Critical-priority goals could override human oversight without constitutional violation, because none is defined.",
      "related_goal_conflict": true,
      "related_msp_section": "MSP §Amendment Protocol — human approval required for MSP amendments, but without BoR, human authority reservation is absent"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-005",
      "source": "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol",
      "description": "The process for amending the BoR is undefined. The only authority specified is 'human principal only,' but no procedural mechanism exists.",
      "priority": "high",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The BoR is locked in scaffold state because no one can amend it; no human or system can complete the constitution. This paralysis perpetuates all other gaps indefinitely.",
      "related_goal_conflict": false,
      "related_msp_section": "MSP Amendment Protocol — describes MSP amendment flow but depends on a functional BoR"
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-bor-006",
      "source": "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules",
      "description": "No rules govern how the BoR should be interpreted. The meaning of future articles would be contested without a defined interpretive framework.",
      "priority": "high",
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "Even after substantive articles are written, interpretation could be inconsistent, undermining constitutional stability. However, this gap is deferred until content exists.",
      "related_goal_conflict": false,
      "related_msp_section": null
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-REL-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights relational principles undefined",
